leadText: Carkol (Japanese: トロッゴン Toroggon) is a dual-type Rock/Fire Pokémon introduced in Generation VIII. It evolves from Rolycoly starting at level 18 and evolves into Coalossal starting at level 34.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Carkol is a Pokémon which resembles a minecart. It has a head with two triangular eyes and a mouth that has a warm, orange glow. Behind its head is a small heap of coal which can glow orange-red, atop a body of black stone. It has four wheel-like legs which it moves on. Carkol forms coal and coal tar inside of its body. Although it frequently drops its coal, the heap on Carkol's back never falls apart completely even when it travels at high speeds, due to the tar binding it together. People in the Galar region once used Carkol's coal to fuel their lives. Carkol can move faster than 18 mph (30 km/h) and can breathe flames hotter than 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit (1,000 degrees Celsius). When Carkol experiences strong emotions, the temperature inside its body increases. Carkol and its evolutionary relatives are the only known Pokémon that can have Steam Engine as an Ability . According to Professor Friede's Pokémon Seminar , the supply of coal that Carkol produces is infinite. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Carkol evolves from Rolycoly and evolves into Coalossal . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Rolycoly Rock → First Evolution Carkol Rock Fire → Second Evolution Coalossal Rock Fire

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Carkol and its evolutionary relatives share their category with Torkoal . They are all known as the Coal Pokémon. Carkol and its evolutionary relatives have several unique attributes: They are the only black Rock-type Pokémon. Carkol and Coalossal are the only Fire-type Pokémon in the Mineral Egg Group . Carkol and Coalossal are the only dual-type Fire/Rock Pokémon to have Rock as their primary type instead of their secondary type. Origin [ edit | edit source ] Carkol appears to be based on a mine cart filled with coal. Its Shiny variant's gray coloration may reference how coal turns white with ash as it burns. Name origin [ edit | edit source ] Carkol may be a combination of car , cart , carbon , and coal or charcoal . Toroggon may be a combination of トロッコ torokko (minecart) and wagon .
